title: Bisphenol A and Adiposity in an Inner-City Birth Cohort.
ehr_evidence: The study uses a prospective birth cohort (CCCEH) with urine samples and anthropometric measurements, not EHR.
summary: A prospective birth cohort study examining the association between prenatal and early-childhood bisphenol A (BPA) exposure and childhood adiposity measures (FMI, %BF, WC) in a New York City birth cohort.
key_findings: Prenatal urinary BPA concentrations were positively associated with child age 7 FMI, %BF, and WC.
Child urinary BPA concentrations were not associated with child anthropometric outcomes.
population: African American and Dominican women and children in Northern Manhattan and the South Bronx, NYC
limitations: Specific to an inner-city birth cohort
data_availability_statement: Associated Data Supplementary Materials(177 KB) PDF Click here for additional data file.
